tex:
(a) 
Calculate the volume of

(i)  a solid cylinder with radius $6\,\text{cm}$ and height $14\,\text{cm}$,

 a solid hemisphere with radius $6\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.

t

The cylinder and hemisphere in **part (a)** are joined to form the solid in the diagram.
\newline

The solid is made of steel and $1\,\text{cm}^3$ of steel has a mass of $7.85\,\text{g}$.
\newline

(i)  Show that $1\,\text{cm}^3$ of steel has a mass of $0.00785\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

 Calculate the total mass of the solid.
\newline

$2000\,\text{cm}^3$ of iron is melted down and some of it is used to make 50 spheres with radius $2\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(i)  Calculate the percentage of iron that is left over.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.
\newline
]

 The iron left over is then made into a cube.
\newline

Calculate the length of an edge of the cube.
\newline

A solid cone has radius $3R\,\text{cm}$ and slant height $9R\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

A solid cylinder has radius $x\,\text{cm}$ and height $7x\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The **total** surface area of the cone is equal to the **total** surface area of the cylinder.
\newline

Given that $R = kx$, find the value of $k$.
\newline

[The curved surface area, $A$, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$.
\newline
]

tex:
(a) 
Write

(i)  2994.99 correct to the nearest 10,

 0.983 correct to 1 decimal place,

 2090 correct to 2 significant figures.
\newline

Write down a prime number between 90 and 100.
\newline

Write $2^{-6}$ as a fraction.
\newline

Write 0.00701 in standard form.
\newline

Simplify $1.5 \times 10^x + 1.5 \times 10^{x-1}$ giving your answer in standard form.
\newline

Write $0.3\dot{7}$ as a fraction.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

qtype: solving linear inequalities, linear equations with fractions
tex:
(a) 
t

Write down the inequality shown by the number line.
\newline

$-3 \leqslant 2x + 3 < 9$

(i)  Solve the inequality.
\newline

 Write down all the integer values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality.
\newline

Solve the equations.
\newline

(i)  $\displaystyle 3(3 - x) - \frac{2(x + 2)}{5} = 1$

 $\displaystyle \frac{5}{x + 3} = \frac{3}{x + 5}$

content.content:
Write down the inequality shown by the number line.
\newline
$-3 \leqslant 2x + 3 < 9$

Solve the inequality.
\newline

Write down all the integer values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality.
\newline

Solve the equations.
\newline
$\displaystyle 3(3 - x) - \frac{2(x + 2)}{5} = 1$
$\displaystyle \frac{5}{x + 3} = \frac{3}{x + 5}$

qtype: simple and compound interest, comparison of investment growth, exponential depreciation
tex:
(a) 
t
(i)  Zak invests \$500 at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of Zak's investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

 Yasmin invests \$500 at a rate of 1.8\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of Yasmin's investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

 Zak and Yasmin continue with these investments.
\newline

How many **more complete** years is it before the value of Yasmin's investment is greater than the value of Zak's investment?

Xavier buys a car for \$2500.
\newline

The value of the car decreases exponentially at a rate of 10\% each year.
\newline

Calculate the value of Xavier's car at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

The number of a certain type of bacteria increases exponentially at a rate of $r$\% each day.
\newline

After 22 days, the number of this bacteria has doubled.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

content.content:
Zak invests \$500 at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of Zak's investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Yasmin invests \$500 at a rate of 1.8\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of Yasmin's investment at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Zak and Yasmin continue with these investments.
\newline

How many \textbf{more complete} years is it before the value of Yasmin's investment is greater than the value of Zak's investment?

Xavier buys a car for \$2500.
\newline

The value of the car decreases exponentially at a rate of 10\% each year.
\newline

Calculate the value of Xavier's car at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

The number of a certain type of bacteria increases exponentially at a rate of $r$\% each day.
\newline

After 22 days, the number of this bacteria has doubled.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

tex:
(a) 
100 students each record the time, $t$ minutes, taken to eat a pizza.
\newline

The cumulative frequency diagram shows the results.
\newline

Find an estimate of

(i)  the median,

 the interquartile range,

 the number of students taking more than 11 minutes to eat a pizza.
\newline

150 students each record how far they can throw a tennis ball.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ metres) & $0 < d \leqslant 20$ & $20 < d \leqslant 30$ & $30 < d \leqslant 35$ & $35 < d \leqslant 45$ & $45 < d \leqslant 60$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 38 & 40 & 53 & 15 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

 A histogram is drawn to show this information.
\newline

The height of the bar representing $30 < d \leqslant 35$ is $12\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the height of each of the other bars.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ metres) & Frequency & Height of bar (cm) \\
\hline
$0 < d \leqslant 20$ & 4 & \\
\hline
$20 < d \leqslant 30$ & 38 & \\
\hline
$30 < d \leqslant 35$ & 40 & 12 \\
\hline
$35 < d \leqslant 45$ & 53 & \\
\hline
$45 < d \leqslant 60$ & 15 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 Two students are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both threw the ball more than $45\,\text{m}$.
\newline

content.content:
100 students each record the time, $t$ minutes, taken to eat a pizza.
\newline

The cumulative frequency diagram shows the results.
\newline
Find an estimate of
the median,
the interquartile range,

the number of students taking more than 11 minutes to eat a pizza.
\newline

150 students each record how far they can throw a tennis ball.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

A histogram is drawn to show this information.
\newline

The height of the bar representing $30 < d \leqslant 35$ is $12\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the height of each of the other bars.
\newline

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ metres) & Frequency & Height of bar (cm) \\
\hline
$0 < d \leqslant 20$ & 4 & \\
\hline
$20 < d \leqslant 30$ & 38 & \\
\hline
$30 < d \leqslant 35$ & 40 & 12 \\
\hline
$35 < d \leqslant 45$ & 53 & \\
\hline
$45 < d \leqslant 60$ & 15 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Two students are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both threw the ball more than $45\,\text{m}$.
\newline

tex:

$\mathbf{p} = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$ \qquad $\mathbf{q} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$

Find

(i)  $3\mathbf{q}$,

 $\mathbf{p} - \mathbf{q}$,

 $|\mathbf{p}|$.
\newline

\newline

Find the coordinates of $A$.

t

In triangle $OGH$, $M$ is the midpoint of $OH$ and $K$ divides $GH$ in the ratio $5:2$.
\newline

$\overrightarrow{OG} = \mathbf{g}$ and $\overrightarrow{OH} = \mathbf{h}$.
\newline

Find $\overrightarrow{MK}$ in terms of $\mathbf{g}$ and $\mathbf{h}$.
\newline

Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

tex:
\[f(x) = 10 - x \qquad g(x) = \frac{2}{x}, \; x \neq 0 \qquad h(x) = 2^x \qquad j(x) = 5 - 2x\]

(a) 
t
(i)  Find $\text{g}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.
\newline

 Find $\text{hg}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{f}(x) = 7$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{g}(x) = \text{h}(3)$.
\newline

Find $\text{j}^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

Write $\text{f}(x) + \text{g}(x) + 1$ as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

$\left(\text{f}(x)\right)^2 - \text{ff}(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$

Find the values of $a$, $b$ and $c$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{h}^{-1}(x) = 10$.
\newline

content.content:
Find $\text{g}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.
\newline

Find $\text{hg}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{f}(x) = 7$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{g}(x) = \text{h}(3)$.
\newline

Find $\text{j}^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

Write $\text{f}(x) + \text{g}(x) + 1$ as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

$\left(\text{f}(x)\right)^2 - \text{ff}(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$

Find the values of $a$, $b$ and $c$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{h}^{-1}(x) = 10$.
\newline

tex:

$AC = 15\,\text{m}$, $BC = 8\,\text{m}$ and $AB = 20\,\text{m}$.
\newline

\newline

\newline

$AQ$ and $PQ$ are straight wires.
\newline

(a) 
Show that angle $ACB = 117.5°$, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

Calculate the area of triangle $ABC$.
\newline

Calculate the length of $AQ$.
\newline

Calculate the angle of elevation of $Q$ from $P$.
\newline

Another straight wire connects $A$ to the midpoint of $PQ$.
\newline

Calculate the angle between this wire and the horizontal ground.
\newline

qtype: graph interpretation, differentiation to find stationary points
tex:
(a) 
t

The diagram shows a sketch of the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

The coordinates of five points on the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ are shown on the diagram.
\newline

(i)  $\text{f}(x) = k$ has two solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

Write down a possible integer value of $k$.
\newline

 $\text{f}(x) = j$ has no solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$ when $j < a$ or $j > b$.
\newline

Find the maximum value of $a$ and the minimum value of $b$.
\newline

Find the coordinates of the two stationary points on the graph of $y = x^6 - 6x^5$.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

{---} ,  )][]
{---} ,  )][]

content.content:
The diagram shows a sketch of the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

The coordinates of five points on the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ are shown on the diagram.
\newline

$\text{f}(x) = k$ has two solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

Write down a possible integer value of $k$.
\newline

$\text{f}(x) = j$ has no solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$ when $j < a$ or $j > b$.
\newline

Find the maximum value of $a$ and the minimum value of $b$.
\newline

Find the coordinates of the two stationary points on the graph of $y = x^6 - 6x^5$.
\newline

You must show all your working.
